zh.Marsh Caundle, Dorset, England: 英國多瑟特考德爾 馬希
zh.St Mary, Whitechapel, Tower Hamlets, Mid: 英國米德萊可堂爾 哈姆勒次
zh.Taunton, Bristol, Massachusetts Bay Colo: 布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼布里斯托陶頓
zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard Burt於1590年出生在英國多瑟特多爾斯特時，他的父親Richard Burt12歲，母親margaret burt20歲。他於1624年11月16日與Jane Adams在英國米德萊可堂爾 哈姆勒次結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1647年10月26日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼布里斯托陶頓享年57歲，埋葬在巴貝多聖 約翰。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Richard Burt于1590年出生在英国多瑟特多爾斯特时，他的父亲Richard Burt12岁，母亲margaret burt20岁。他于1624年11月16日与Jane Adams在英国米德萊可堂爾 哈姆勒次结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1647年10月26日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼布里斯托陶頓享年57岁，埋葬在巴贝多聖 約翰。

nameMeanings.name: Burt
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (southern): variant of   Bird  ,   Bright  , or   Burd  .
German: Middle High German burt ‘that which is due or proper’, therefore a nickname for someone who has fulfilled his obligations properly.
Jewish (from Poland and Ukraine): variant of   Burd  .

variants.name: Bird
meanings: English and Scottish: nickname for a young or a small and slender person, from Middle English brid, bird, burd (Old English bird, brid, perhaps also byrd) ‘bird, young bird’, also ‘young man, young woman, child’.

Irish: Anglicized form of a number of Irish names erroneously thought to contain the element éan ‘bird’, in particular Ó hÉinigh (see Heagney ), Ó hÉanna (see Heaney ), Ó hÉanacháin (see Heneghan ), and Mac an Déaghanaigh (see McEneaney ).

Americanized form (translation into English) of various European surnames meaning ‘bird’, as for example German and Jewish (Ashkenazic) Vogel , French Loiseau , Czech Ptáček (see Ptacek ) and Pták, Polish Ptak .

variants.name: Birt
meanings: German: habitational name from any of several places in the Rhineland named Birth or Birten.
In some cases also Croatian: occupational name from birt ‘inn-keeper’, a regional or colloquial word of German origin (see Wirt ).

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: When Richard Burt was born in 1590, in Dorchester, Dorset, England, his father, Richard Burt, was 12 and his mother, margaret burt, was 20. He married Jane Adams on 16 November 1624, in Tower Hamlets, Middlesex, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 2 daughters. He died on 26 October 1647, in Taunton, Bristol, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, at the age of 57, and was buried in Saint John, Barbados.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard Burt est né en 1590, à Dorchester, Dorset, Angleterre, son père, Richard Burt, avait 12 ans et sa mère, margaret burt, avait 20 ans. Il a épousé Jane Adams le 16 novembre 1624, à Tower Hamlets, Middlesex, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé le 26 octobre 1647, à Taunton, Bristol, Massachusetts Bay Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique, à l’âge de 57 ans, et a été enterré à Saint John, Barbade.

ja.lifeSummary: Richard Burtが1590年にイギリスドールセトドールチェスターで生まれた時，父Richard Burtは12歳，母margaret burtは20歳であった。1624年11月16日にイギリスミッドレセックストウエル ハムレツでJane Adamsと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1647年10月26日にイギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニーブリストルタウントンで57歳で死去し，バルバドスサイント ジョンにて埋葬された。

ko.Taunton, Bristol, Massachusetts Bay Colo: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 브리스톨 타운턴
ko.lifeSummary: Richard Burt(이)가 1590년 영국 도르세트 도르체스터에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Richard Burt(은)는 12세였고, 그의 어머니 margaret burt(은)는 20세였다. 그는 1624년 11월 16일 영국 미들레세스 타워 함레츠에서 Jane Adams(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1647년 10월 26일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 브리스톨 타운턴에서 57세의 나이로 사망했으며 바베이도스 세인트 존에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Richard Burt nasceu em 1590, em Dorchester, Dorset, Inglaterra, seu pai, Richard Burt, tinha 12 anos e sua mãe, margaret burt, tinha 20 anos. Ele casou-se com Jane Adams em 16 de novembro de 1624, em Tower Hamlets, Middlesex, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em 26 de outubro de 1647, em Taunton, Bristol, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, com 57 anos, e foi sepultado em Saint John, Barbados.

ru.Taunton, Bristol, Massachusetts Bay Colo: таунтон, бристол, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard Burt родился в 1590 г. в местности дорчестир, дорсет, Англия, его отцу по имени Richard Burt было 12 лет, а матери, которую звали margaret burt, 20. Он женился на женщине по имени Jane Adams 16 ноября 1624 г. в местности тоуир хамлец, миддлесекс, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 2 дочери. Он умер 26 октября 1647 г. в местности таунтон, бристол, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 57 лет и был похоронен в местности сеинт джон, Барбадос.
